sections.paragraph:
ναύκληρος, ου, ὁ (ναῦς, κλῆρος; Soph., Hdt.+; Plut., Jos. [both w. κυβερνήτης, q.v. 1]; OGI 344, 4 [I b.c.]; pap [Preisigke, Fachwörter; for others New Docs, below; En 101:4, 9; Philo, Op. M. 147) freq. ‘ship-owner’ or ‘charterer’ of a vessel. But it can also mean captain, since the sailing-master of a ship engaged in state service (esp. for shipment of grain IG 14, 918) was called a ναύκληρος (MRostovtzeff, APF 5, 1913, 298; LCasson, Ships and Seamanship in the Ancient World, ’71, 314–16; but WKunkel, APF 8, ’27, 185 ‘freight contractor’, not necessarily as pilot or captain) Ac 27:11. (For sailing regulations POxy 3250, 22–24 [63 a.d.], s. New Docs 2, 74 no. 25.)—Nock, Essays 823; Hemer, Acts 138f; JVélissaropoulos, Les nauclères grecs, ’80; New Docs 4, 116f (pap, ins and lit.).—DELG. M-M. Spicq.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Preisigke Preisigke = FP.—Lists 4, 6

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

esp. esp. = especially

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Nock Nock = AN., Essays on Religion and the Ancient World—List 6

Hemer, Acts Hemer, Acts = CH., The Book of Acts in the Setting of Hellenistic History—List 6

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 667
